blocks.body: Present Perfect Continuous: forma i użycie — have / has been + V-ing dla procesu, długości trwania do teraz i niedawnej aktywności z widocznym rezultatem.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"W Present Perfect Continuous używamy have / has been + V-ing, gdy chcemy pokazać czynność, która zaczęła się wcześniej i trwa do teraz albo właśnie się skończyła z widocznym rezultatem.","rules":[{"label":"have / has been + V-ing","color":"emerald","text":"To podstawowy model twierdzący tego czasu.","example":"I have been working. / She has been waiting."},{"label":"Started before now","color":"blue","text":"Ten czas dobrze działa, gdy czynność zaczęła się w przeszłości i nie zakończyła się do chwili obecnej.","example":"We have been living here since 2022. / He has been studying for two hours."},{"label":"Focus on process","color":"amber","text":"Akcent często pada na proces albo długość trwania, a nie tylko na gotowy rezultat.","example":"I have been reading all morning. / They have been talking lately."}]}

{"title":"1. Podstawowy wzór","intro":"Zapamiętaj formę dla każdej grupy podmiotów.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"have been + V-ing","subtitle":"I have been working. / They have been waiting."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"has been + V-ing","subtitle":"She has been studying. / It has been raining."},{"label":"Spelling","title":"work → working, live → living, run → running","subtitle":"Po been użyj czasownika z -ing."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"process / duration / recent activity","subtitle":"Czynność trwa do teraz albo właśnie się skończyła z widocznym rezultatem."}]}

{"title":"2. Kiedy używamy Present Perfect Continuous","sections":[{"label":"Czynność nadal trwa","color":"emerald","description":"Czynność zaczęła się w przeszłości i nadal trwa.","examples":[{"en":"I have been learning English for six months.","ua":"Czynność nadal trwa."},{"en":"She has been waiting since 8 a.m.","ua":"Czynność zaczęła się wcześniej i wciąż trwa."}]},{"label":"Długość trwania do teraz","color":"blue","description":"Ten czas podkreśla, jak długo trwa proces.","examples":[{"en":"We have been working all morning.","ua":"Długość trwania jest ważna."},{"en":"He has been driving for three hours.","ua":"Proces trwa już przez pewien czas."}]},{"label":"Niedawna aktywność z widocznym rezultatem","color":"amber","description":"Czynność mogła się właśnie skończyć, ale rezultat widać teraz.","examples":[{"en":"She is tired because she has been cleaning.","ua":"Niedawny proces wyjaśnia obecny rezultat."},{"en":"Your hands are dirty. Have you been gardening?","ua":"Rezultat widać teraz."}]},{"label":"Niezakończony okres","color":"violet","description":"Ten czas często pojawia się z today albo this week, gdy okres jeszcze się nie skończył.","examples":[{"en":"I have been reading this article today.","ua":"Dzisiejszy dzień nadal trwa."},{"en":"They have been meeting a lot this week.","ua":"Ten tydzień jeszcze się nie skończył."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Szybkie sprawdzenie formy","intro":"Zobacz, jak have / has łączy się z been i V-ing.","rows":[{"en":"I have been studying for two hours.","ua":"have been studying","note":"I + have been + V-ing"},{"en":"She has been working since morning.","ua":"has been working","note":"she + has been + V-ing"},{"en":"We have been waiting all day.","ua":"have been waiting","note":"we + have been + V-ing"},{"en":"He has been running lately.","ua":"has been running","note":"he + has been + V-ing"}],"warning":"Nie mów She has been work ani I have studying."}

{"title":"4. Typowe błędy","items":[{"label":"Błąd 1","color":"rose","title":"Pomijanie been w formule czasu.","wrong":"❌ I have working all day.","right":"✅ I have been working all day."},{"label":"Błąd 2","color":"amber","title":"Używanie V1 albo V3 zamiast V-ing.","wrong":"❌ She has been cleaned the room.","right":"✅ She has been cleaning the room."},{"label":"Błąd 3","color":"sky","title":"Używanie tego czasu ze stative verbs, gdy potrzebny jest inny czas.","wrong":"❌ I have been knowing him for years.","right":"✅ I have known him for years."}]}

{"title":"5. Krótkie podsumowanie","items":["Present Perfect Continuous = have / has been + V-ing.","Używaj have been z I / you / we / they.","Używaj has been z he / she / it.","Ten czas podkreśla proces, długość trwania albo niedawną aktywność z widocznym rezultatem.","Po been używaj czasownika z -ing."]}

{"title":"Lekcje Present Perfect Continuous","items":[{"label":"Forma i użycie","url":"/pl/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-forms","current":true},{"label":"Przeczenia","url":"/pl/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-negatives","current":false},{"label":"Pytania i krótkie odpowiedzi","url":"/pl/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-questions","current":false},{"label":"Określenia czasu","url":"/pl/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-time-expressions","current":false}]}
